turns.title: Initial task: extract objecttypes and match to fabio subclasses
turns.user:
1. read ddb-search-goethe-all.json
2. take all the values of response → docs → objecttype into a list of dict, where key is the doc id, and the list consists of all the objecttype values per doc
3. take all the subclasses of fabio:Work, fabio:Expression, fabio:Manifestation, fabio:Item from fabio.owl
4. try to match the objecttype with the subclass names
5. Save result in ddb-type2fabio.json
turns.assistant: Parsed ddb-search-goethe-all.json (97,172 docs) and fabio.owl using xml.etree.ElementTree. Found 94 fabio subclasses (30 Work, 57 Expression, 4 Manifestation, 3 Item). Applied case-insensitive exact matching on normalized class names and labels.

turns.title: Add German translation + approximate matching (Levenshtein + embeddings)
turns.user:
Revise the matching script:
1. translate German terms to English
2. match translated terms to fabio subclasses
3. for unmatched terms: use approximate matching
   3.1 levenshtein distance of 2
   3.2 word embeddings
4. for approximate matching, indicate confidence score
5. save matching criteria: strict, levenshtein, embeddings
turns.assistant: Created Python venv and installed deep-translator, rapidfuzz, sentence-transformers. Implemented 4-tier pipeline: (1) strict exact match, (2) strict on Google-translated term, (3) Levenshtein distance ≤ 2 with confidence = 1 - dist/max_len, (4) sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2 cosine similarity ≥ 0.55.

pipeline.description: Case-insensitive exact match on class name, CamelCase-split words, and rdfs:labels
Translate via Google Translate (DE→EN), then exact match
